model.signal:
<!-- index.html, inside the embedded <galath> source --> <galath xmlns:bind="urn:galath:bind" xmlns:on="urn:galath:on" xmlns:use="urn:galath:use" xmlns:drag="urn:galath:drag" xmlns:drop="urn:galath:drop" xmlns:class="urn:galath:class"> <import src="./playground/components/chapter-shell.xml" /> <import src="./playground/chapters/01-welcome.xml" /> <import src="./playground/chapters/02-signals.xml" /> <!-- ...one line per chapter... --> <import src="./playground/app.xml" />
<application name="playground"> <x-playground /> </application> </galath>
Welcome

x-chapter-heading.title: Imports
x-chapter-heading.subtitle: Split a Galath app across files. The same way ES modules split a JS app.

div.p: An <import src="./path/to/file.xml"/> element is replaced at boot time by the children of the imported file's root. The import is fetched asynchronously, the result is cached by URL, and nested imports inside the imported file are resolved relative to that file's URL.
The fetched file's root can be:

ul.li: <galath> or <fragment> - splices in its children. Use this when one file ships several components.
Anything else - splices in the root element itself. Use this when one file is one component.

div.p: Cycles are detected by URL and broken silently. If a fetch fails, a <parseerror> element appears in place so you can spot the problem.

Every chapter you've clicked through is a separate .xml file under ./playground/chapters/. The shared chapter heading is in ./playground/components/. Open index.html to see how it all wires together - it's a small file because the work is delegated to imports.
Rule of thumb: when an XML file gets long enough that you start scrolling to find things, break it into imports. Same heuristic as JS modules.
